title: 5 times the Patriots traded for wide receivers in recent history, and how each acquisition panned out
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Randy Moss set an NFL record with 23 touchdowns in 2007 after being traded from the Raiders.
Wes Welker became a prolific slot receiver for the Patriots after being acquired from the Dolphins in 2007.
Josh Gordon showed potential with over 700 yards in 2018, but off-field issues ended his Patriots tenure early.

title: Alex Cora not harping on late-game ejection by C.B. Bucknor: ‘We’re all human’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora was ejected in the eighth inning for arguing a check swing strike call against Trevor Story.
Home plate umpire C.B. Bucknor decided against consulting the first base umpire on the contested strike call.
Despite the ejection, Cora emphasized understanding the pressure on umpires and aims to use the incident as a learning experience.

title: Trevor Story reacts to strike three call that got Alex Cora ejected in Red Sox’ loss
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Trevor Story objected to umpire CB Bucknor's strike three call without consulting the first base ump, leading to Alex Cora's ejection.
Story expressed strong disagreement postgame, stating many disagreed with Bucknor's decision during the Red Sox's 6-5 loss.
Despite Story's ninth-inning strikeout, the Red Sox tied the game before losing to the Twins in the 11th inning.

title: Brimming with motivation after missing Olympics, Pavel Zacha is playing his best hockey for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Pavel Zacha scored 11 goals and 19 points in 16 games post-Olympic break for the Boston Bruins.
Zacha missed the 2026 Olympics due to concussion from a hit by Nicolas Deslauriers, motivating his recent performance.
Zacha surpassed his career-high goals with 26 this season, aiding Bruins as second-line center in a playoff push.

title: Here’s what Red Sox SP Sonny Gray said about his rough debut
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray allowed four runs in four innings in his Red Sox debut against the Cincinnati Reds.
Manager Alex Cora noted the defensive support was lacking despite Gray settling in after the first inning.
The Boston bullpen posted scoreless innings until losing in the 11th on Dane Myers's walk-off hit.

title: Tens of thousands rally at Boston Common for third ‘No Kings’ protest
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Tens of thousands gathered at Boston Common for the third 'No Kings' rally organized by ACLU of Massachusetts and other groups.
The event, part of nationwide protests, featured speeches by leaders like Gov. Maura Healey and performances by the Dropkick Murphys.
Over 3,300 rallies occurred nationwide with more than 160 in Massachusetts, addressing issues like immigration, military involvement, and democracy.

title: Review & setlist: GWAR gives fanbase a bloody good time at House of Blues
author: Christopher Hurley
bullets: GWAR delivered a 90-minute, 17-song set at Boston's House of Blues with theatrical shock rock antics.
The performance featured fake blood, satirical sketches, and a cover of Chappell Roan's 'Pink Pony Club'.
The concert included parodies of political figures like Donald Trump and Kristi Noem amid aggressive stage theatrics.

title: Celtics post lengthy injury report ahead of Sunday’s Hornets matchup
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum are questionable for the Celtics' game against the Charlotte Hornets due to Achilles issues.
Neemias Queta and Derrick White join Brown and Tatum on the questionable list; Nikola Vucevic is out with a finger fracture.
Despite performance struggles, Jayson Tatum's defensive rebounding has been crucial, contributing to the Celtics' recent victories.

title: ‘No Kings’ protest: What protesters said and why they showed up
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Thousands gathered in Boston Common for the 'No Kings' protest, part of over 160 rallies across Massachusetts.
Speakers at the Boston rally included Gov. Maura Healey, Sen. Elizabeth Warren, and performances by Dropkick Murphys.
Protesters, including veterans and church groups, expressed concerns about democracy, global tensions, and the rise of concentrated power.

title: Photo scroll: Scenes from Boston’s ‘No Kings’ protest on March 28
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Thousands gathered for the 'No Kings' protest at Boston Common on March 28, part of 160 events in Massachusetts.
Governor Maura Healey, Senators Edward Markey and Elizabeth Warren addressed the protestors at the Boston rally.
The protest was part of a nationwide movement against monarchy, featuring rallies across the U.S. on March 28, 2026.
